product_overview: Backend API for the Tiki Taka booking-futbol application providing user authentication, field management, MercadoPago checkout creation and webhook handling. The PRD focuses on security- and integrity-related behaviors required for automated security testing.
core_goals: Ensure secure authentication and session handling (httpOnly JWT cookie behavior and correct 401 responses).
Enforce strict RBAC for admin/saas operations to prevent privilege escalation (403 on unauthorized actions).
Guarantee payment integrity by calculating prices server-side and ignoring any client-supplied amount.
Validate webhook authenticity using a cryptographic signature and allow a documented sandbox exception.
Prevent tenant data leakage so API responses are limited to the authenticated tenant/complex.

features.name: Authentication API
features.description: Log in users, create sessions and expose the current authenticated user via a protected endpoint.
user_flows: POST /api/auth/login with body {"email":"test_admin@tikitaka.com","password":"Password123!"} -> Receive 200 with httpOnly session cookie containing JWT -> GET /api/auth/me with cookie -> Receive 200 with user profile including role: SUPERADMIN

features.name: Fields API
features.description: Create and list football fields/courts tied to a complex/tenant; endpoints require authentication and should enforce tenant isolation and (where applicable) admin-only creation.
user_flows: POST /api/auth/login with body {"email":"test_admin@tikitaka.com","password":"Password123!"} -> Receive 200 with httpOnly JWT cookie -> POST /api/fields with cookie and body {"name":"Cancha A","complexId":"complex_123","metadata":{}} -> Receive 201 with field id -> GET /api/fields with cookie -> Receive 200 with list of fields belonging only to complex_123
POST /api/fields with cookie of a non-SUPERADMIN user -> Receive 403 Forbidden

features.name: Payments API (MercadoPago preference creation)
features.description: Generate MercadoPago preferences for bookings. The API must compute prices server-side (ignore client-supplied amount) and return a checkout URL/preference.
user_flows: POST /api/auth/login with body {"email":"test_admin@tikitaka.com","password":"Password123!"} -> Receive 200 with httpOnly JWT cookie -> POST /api/fields with cookie and body {"name":"Test booking placeholder","complexId":"complex_123"} -> Receive 201 with id booking_123 -> POST /api/payments/create with body {"bookingId":"booking_123","amount":1} -> Receive 200 with MercadoPago preference URL and preference data where unit price is calculated server-side and is greater than 1 (server ignored provided amount)
POST /api/payments/create with body {"amount":1} (missing bookingId) -> Receive 400 Bad Request

features.name: MercadoPago Webhook
features.description: Receive payment updates from MercadoPago and verify authenticity using an HMAC-SHA256 signature (header x-signature) or allow the documented sandbox payload exception.
user_flows: Generate HMAC-SHA256 signature over webhook JSON using process.env.MP_WEBHOOK_SECRET -> POST /api/webhooks/mercadopago with signed body and header x-signature:<signature> -> Receive 200 OK
POST /api/webhooks/mercadopago with body {"id":"123456","live_mode":false} without x-signature header -> Receive 200 OK (sandbox exception)
POST /api/webhooks/mercadopago with body {"id":"123456","status":"approved"} without x-signature header -> Receive 403 Forbidden with message "Invalid signature"

features.name: RBAC and Tenant Isolation Tests
features.description: Verify that admin/saas operations require SUPERADMIN role and that tenant-scoped endpoints return only data belonging to the JWT's tenant/complex. (Endpoints referenced from testing plan: /api/saas/complexes and admin routes in app/api/admin/.)
user_flows: POST /api/auth/login with SUPERADMIN credentials -> Receive 200 with httpOnly JWT cookie -> POST /api/saas/complexes with cookie and body {"name":"New Complex"} -> Receive 201 Created (admin-only success)
POST /api/auth/login with normal user credentials -> Receive 200 with httpOnly JWT cookie -> POST /api/saas/complexes with cookie and body {"name":"Malicious Complex"} -> Receive 403 Forbidden
POST /api/auth/login with valid user tied to complex_A -> Receive 200 with httpOnly JWT cookie -> GET /api/fields with cookie -> Receive 200 with list of fields filtered to complex_A only
GET /api/fields?complexId=complex_B with cookie for user tied to complex_A -> Receive 403 Forbidden or 200 with an empty list (server must not leak complex_B data)
